title: galium serpenticum subsp. scabridum
intro: Galium serpenticum subsp. scabridum, also known as Rough-leaved Bedstraw, is a perennial herb belonging to the Rubiaceae family. The plant has been traditionally used for its medicinal properties, particularly for its antiseptic and anti-inflammatory effects, which are attributed to the presence of flavonoids, phenolic acids, and other bioactive compounds. The health benefits of Galium serpenticum subsp. scabridum include its potential to soothe skin irritations, reduce fever, and support the immune system, making it a valuable herb in traditional medicine. Herbal preparations of Galium serpenticum subsp. scabridum can be made into teas, infusions, or tinctures for topical and internal use, although its effectiveness and safety should be further researched and validated.
